header_info.title: Global Oil and Gas Extraction Tracker - March 2026
header_info.contact: Contact: Scott Zimmerman, Global Oil and Gas Extraction Tracker Project Manager, Global Energy Monitor - scott.zimmerman@globalenergymonitor.org

entries.name: Unit ID
entries.definition: Unique Unit ID number. Reformatted for this release.  Users can convert from the old format to the new by appending L1000003 to the right five digits from the old IDs.

entries.name: Unit Name
entries.definition: Name of the unit as it is reported by sources, appended to fuel type and country/area information.

entries.name: Unit name local script
entries.definition: The name of the unit in the local script (e.g., Chinese).

entries.name: Fuel type
entries.definition: A determination of whether the unit is a gas, oil, or oil and gas unit.

entries.name: Country/Area
entries.definition: The country/area that the unit is in. If a unit spans country borders, both countries are listed in the Unit name.

entries.name: Subnational unit
entries.definition: The subnational state that the unit is in (province, state, etc.).

entries.name: Production Type
entries.definition: Tag stating if production from a field uses conventional or unconventional methods.

entries.name: Status
entries.definition: The status of development of a unit, e.g., discovered, in development, operating, mothballed, decommissioned, abandoned, or UGS.

entries.name: Status detail
entries.definition: Additional information on the status, see methodology for details.

entries.name: Status year
entries.definition: The year of the source of the status information.

entries.name: Discovery year
entries.definition: Year in which the unit was discovered.

entries.name: FID Year
entries.definition: Year in which the unit reach final investment decision.

entries.name: Production start year
entries.definition: Year in which the unit started commercial production.

entries.name: Operator
entries.definition: The company that is the designated operator of the unit.

entries.name: Owner(s)
entries.definition: The company (or companies) that own the unit. Often multiple companies will collaborate to fund a project.

entries.name: Parent(s)
entries.definition: The company (or companies) that own the owner companies listed.

entries.name: Government unit ID
entries.definition: The ID assigned by some governments to the asset.

entries.name: Wiki URL (project)
entries.definition: URL for the GEM.wiki page for an associated project, if relevant.

entries.name: Wiki URL (field)
entries.definition: URL for the GEM.wiki page for the field-level unit.

entries.name: Name Other
entries.definition: Alternate names for a field-level unit

entries.name: Latitude
entries.definition: Location of the unit. Since each unit spans some area (often many miles), the coordinates provided are approximately the middle of the unit.

entries.name: Longitude
entries.definition: Location of the unit. Since each unit spans some area (often many miles), the coordinates provided are approximately the middle of the unit.

entries.name: Location accuracy
entries.definition: Locations are designated as "exact" if there is information about where exactly the unit is (e.g., coordinates explicitly stated, or unit shown on a map). If only general information is available (e.g., which state or county the unit is in), an "approximate" designation is used.

entries.name: Onshore/Offshore
entries.definition: Tag stating if a field predominantly located on or offshore

entries.name: Field outline (WKT)
entries.definition: Field-level units outline in well-known-text format.

entries.name: Basin
entries.definition: Each field-level unit is within a particular basin (short for sedimentary basin), when available, shown here.

entries.name: Block
entries.definition: Each unit is generally within a single concession or block, which is an area that the government licenses for exploration, when available, shown here.

entries.name: Unit ID
entries.definition: Unique Unit ID number

entries.name: Unit Name
entries.definition: Name of the unit as it is reported by sources.

entries.name: Country/Area
entries.definition: The country/area that the unit is in. If a unit spans country borders, both countries are listed in the Unit name.

entries.name: Fuel description
entries.definition: Specifies the type of fuel(s), e.g., "gas," "oil," "gas and condensates," "total hydrocarbons," and other possibilities that may be specified by the source documents

entries.name: Reserves classification
entries.definition: For reserves only, a description of the classification of reserves, e.g., 1P reserves, 2P reserves, proved reserves, EUR, or other possibilities (as specified by the source documents)

entries.name: Quantity (original)
entries.definition: The quantity of fuel, as specified by the source documents

entries.name: Units (original)
entries.definition: The units of measurement, as specified by the source documents

entries.name: Quantity (converted)
entries.definition: The quantity after the conversion of data into a common set of units.

entries.name: Units (converted)
entries.definition: The common set of units.

entries.name: Data Year
entries.definition: Year the data refers to, or if not listed, year of the source document

entries.name: Project ID
entries.definition: Unique project ID number. Reformatted for this release.  Users can convert from the old format to the new by appending L1000003 to the right five digits from the old IDs.

entries.name: Project Name
entries.definition: Name of the project as it is reported by sources, appended to fuel type and country/area information.

entries.name: Unit name local script
entries.definition: The name of the project in the local script (e.g., Chinese).

entries.name: Fuel type
entries.definition: A determination of whether the project is a gas, oil, or oil and gas project.

entries.name: Country/Area
entries.definition: The country/area that the project is in. If a project spans country borders, both countries are listed in the project name.

entries.name: Subnational unit
entries.definition: The subnational state that the project is in (province, state, etc.).

entries.name: Production Type
entries.definition: Tag stating if production from a field uses conventional or unconventional methods.

entries.name: Status
entries.definition: The status of development of a project, e.g., discovered, in development, operating, mothballed, decommissioned, abandoned, or UGS.

entries.name: Status detail
entries.definition: Additional information on the status, see methodology for details.

entries.name: Status year
entries.definition: The year of the source of the status information.

entries.name: Discovery year
entries.definition: Year in which the project was discovered. If the project is made up of multiple fields, the earliest discovery year is listed here.

entries.name: FID Year
entries.definition: Year in which the project reach final investment decision. If the project is made up of multiple fields, the earliest FID year is listed here.

entries.name: Production start year
entries.definition: Year in which the project started commercial production.If the project is made up of multiple fields, the earliest FID year is listed here.

entries.name: Operator
entries.definition: The company that is the designated operator of the project.

entries.name: Owner(s)
entries.definition: The company (or companies) that own the project. Often multiple companies will collaborate to fund a project.

entries.name: Parent(s)
entries.definition: The company (or companies) that own the owner companies listed.

entries.name: Government unit ID
entries.definition: The ID assigned by some governments to the asset.

entries.name: Units (list of IDs)
entries.definition: Project IDs of all field-level projects that make up the project.

entries.name: Wiki URL (project)
entries.definition: URL for the GEM.wiki page for an associated project, if relevant.

entries.name: Name Other
entries.definition: Alternate names for a project-level project.

entries.name: Latitude
entries.definition: Location of the project. Since each project spans some area (often many miles), the coordinates provided are approximately the middle of the project.

entries.name: Longitude
entries.definition: Location of the project. Since each project spans some area (often many miles), the coordinates provided are approximately the middle of the project.

entries.name: Location accuracy
entries.definition: Locations are designated as "exact" if there is information about where exactly the project is (e.g., coordinates explicitly stated, or project shown on a map). If only general information  is available (e.g., which state or county the project is in), an "approximate" designation is used.

entries.name: Project location type
entries.definition: If the location of the project is calculated from the location of the field-level projects which make it up, it is tagged as "location derived from fields". If data for the location is at the project level, this is tagged as "project level location".

entries.name: Onshore/Offshore
entries.definition: Tag stating if a field predominantly located on or offshore.

entries.name: Project outline (WKT)
entries.definition: Field-level projects outline in well-known-text format.

entries.name: Basin
entries.definition: Each field-level project is within a particular basin (short for sedimentary basin), when available, shown here.

entries.name: Block
entries.definition: Each project is generally within a single concession or block, which is an area that the government licenses for exploration, when available, shown here.

entries.name: Project ID
entries.definition: Unique project ID number.

entries.name: Project Name
entries.definition: Name of the unit as it is reported by sources.

entries.name: Country/Area
entries.definition: The country/area that the unit is in. If a unit spans country borders, both countries are listed in the Unit name.

entries.name: Fuel description
entries.definition: Specifies the type of fuel(s), e.g., "gas," "oil," "gas and condensates," "total hydrocarbons," and other possibilities that may be specified by the source documents.

entries.name: Reserves classification
entries.definition: For reserves only, a description of the classification of reserves, e.g., 1P reserves, 2P reserves, proved reserves, EUR, or other possibilities (as specified by the source documents)

entries.name: Quantity (original)
entries.definition: The quantity of fuel, as specified by the source documents.

entries.name: Units (original)
entries.definition: The units of measurement, as specified by the source documents.

entries.name: Quantity (converted)
entries.definition: The quantity after the conversion of data into a common set of units.

entries.name: Units (converted)
entries.definition: The common set of units.

entries.name: Data source type
entries.definition: If the reserves of the project is calculated from the the field-level projects which make it up, it is tagged as "reserves summed from fields". If data for the location is at the project level, this is tagged as "project level reserves" - if both data types are available, only the project level data is shown here.

entries.name: Project ID
entries.definition: Unique project ID number.

entries.name: Project Name
entries.definition: Name of the unit as it is reported by sources.

entries.name: Country/Area
entries.definition: The country/area that the unit is in. If a unit spans country borders, both countries are listed in the Unit name.

entries.name: Fuel description
entries.definition: Specifies the type of fuel(s), e.g., "gas," "oil," "gas and condensates," "total hydrocarbons," and other possibilities that may be specified by the source documents.

entries.name: Quantity (converted)
entries.definition: The quantity after the conversion of data into a common set of units.

entries.name: Units (converted)
entries.definition: The common set of units.

entries.name: Data source type
entries.definition: If the production of the project is calculated from the the field-level projects which make it up, it is tagged as "production summed from fields". If data for the location is at the project level, this is tagged as "project level production" - if both data types are available, only the project level data is shown here.

entries.name: Discovered
entries.definition: A unit is typically considered to be discovered when there has been a well drilled into a particular formation, and it yielded some significant quantity of oil and/or gas. Discovered units are not necessarily economic to exploit.

entries.name: In-development
entries.definition: Following discovery, the process of moving toward commercial production has begun. This means that a company is planning to develop the project, as evidenced by one or more of the following criteria being reached: the company has applied for approval for commercial production (if needed in the jurisdiction), the project has reached the Final Investment Decision (FID), a final environmental impact statement has been published, and/or the drilling of development (not appraisal) wells and/or adding takeaway capacity (infrastructure such as pipelines, storage tanks, etc.) to enable commercial production has begun.

entries.name: Operating
entries.definition: The unit has commercial production of oil/gas (that is, quantities that are being sold to market).

entries.name: Abandoned
entries.definition: Operations have ceased, but the wells/unit were not decommissioned following all the usual steps, such as plugging the wells (including filling the well with cement).

entries.name: Mothballed
entries.definition: Operations temporarily shut down; may be restarted.

entries.name: Decommissioning
entries.definition: Operations permanently ceased; infrastructure is typically removed.

entries.name: UGS
entries.definition: Exhausted units may be converted from producing to serving as underground gas storage (UGS) facilities; these are labeled as “UGS.”

entries.name: Exploration
entries.definition: An unproven concessionary block or region that potentially contains an undiscovered field is actively being explored for reserves.

entries.name: Cancelled
entries.definition: A discovered or in-development field that did not reach commercial production. This is evidenced by a formal acknowledgment by a company involved that a project is not going forward.

entries.name: Underground gas storage
entries.definition: Exhausted fields may be converted from producing to serving as underground gas storage (UGS) facilities; these are labeled as “UGS” or “UGS (converted).”
